steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 2:
$$p = \frac{50}{2} = 25$$
That matches choice B.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Subtracting $275$ from both sides of the given equation yields $2p=50$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $2$ yields $p=25$. Therefore, the value of $p$ that satisfies the given equation is $25$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $p$ that satisfies the equation $\left(2+p\right)+275=325$, not $2p+275=325$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $p$ that satisfies the equation $2p-275=325$, not $2p+275=325$.
